title: Urachne haenkeana
taxonomy: Urachne haenkeana belongs to the family Thymelaeaceae. It is a genus of flowering plants native to New Zealand. The classification of Urachne haenkeana is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, division - Magnoliophyta, class - Magnoliopsida, order - Malvales, family - Thymelaeaceae, genus - Urachne, species - U. haenkeana. This plant is one of the six species within the genus Urachne. Further classification of Urachne haenkeana is as follows: tribe - Thymelaeae.

intro: Urachne haenkeana, also known as the Haenke's grass or Urachne, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Urachne haenkeana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Urachne haenkeana is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
